footer.description: Cameroon's first Python conference, bringing together developers, enthusiasts, and innovators from across Africa and beyond.

home.heroSubtitle: Africa's Python Conference, Reimagined in the Heart of Africa
home.welcomeText: Join us for Cameroon's first-ever PyCon — a celebration of Python, technology, and African innovation. Experience two days of inspiring talks, hands-on workshops, and unforgettable networking opportunities in the vibrant city of Yaoundé.
home.card1Text: Connect with developers, entrepreneurs, and tech enthusiasts from across Africa and the world. Experience the unique blend of Cameroonian culture and cutting-edge technology.
home.card2Text: Whether you're a beginner taking your first steps or an expert pushing boundaries, our diverse program caters to all skill levels and interests.
home.card3Text: We believe in building a welcoming, inclusive community where everyone feels valued. Diversity and collaboration are at the heart of everything we do.
home.inspiringTalksText: From beginner to expert, our diverse range of talks covers web development, data science, machine learning, DevOps, and more.
home.workshopsText: Learn by doing with practical workshops led by industry experts. Build real projects and gain valuable skills.
home.networkingText: Connect with fellow Pythonistas, potential employers, and collaborators in a friendly, supportive environment.
home.funActivitiesText: Lightning talks, sprints, cultural experiences, and social events make PyCon Cameroon an unforgettable experience.
home.ubuconDesc1Start: For the first time, PyCon Cameroon 2026 is partnering with
home.ubuconDesc1End: to bring you a dedicated Ubuntu and open source track! This special track is co-organized by Python Cameroon and UbuCon Cameroon.
home.ubuconDesc2: Explore talks and workshops on Ubuntu, Linux system administration, open source contributions, cloud infrastructure, and how Python powers the Ubuntu ecosystem.
home.djangoDesc1End: for web developers! Dive into the world of Django — from building robust web applications to REST APIs, deployment best practices, and scaling Django in production.
home.djangoDesc2: Whether you're just starting with Django or you're a seasoned developer, this track offers hands-on workshops, real-world case studies, and expert talks.
home.readyToJoinText: Be part of Cameroon's first PyCon and help shape the future of Python in Africa.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Discover who we are and our mission to grow the Python community in Cameroon
about.userGroupP1: The Python Cameroon User Group is a vibrant community of software developers, data scientists, and tech enthusiasts in Cameroon who are passionate about the Python programming language. We are dedicated to sharing knowledge, fostering innovation, and promoting the adoption of Python across all sectors.
about.userGroupP2: Our community meets regularly to discuss various topics related to Python, including web development with Django and Flask, scientific computing, machine learning with TensorFlow and PyTorch, data science with Pandas, automation, DevOps, and much more.
about.userGroupP3: We organize speaker sessions, coding workshops, hackathons, and mentorship programs to help members improve their Python skills and build practical applications that solve real-world problems.
about.missionP1: PyCon Cameroon is a community-driven conference that aims to provide a platform for the growth of the Python community in Cameroon and across Central Africa. We believe that diversity and inclusivity are essential for the growth and success of our community.
about.missionP2: We strive to create a welcoming and inclusive environment for all attendees, regardless of their backgrounds, genders, ages, or identities. Our conference is designed to inspire, educate, and connect developers at every skill level.
about.missionP3: Bringing together techies from across Africa and the globe, startups, students, researchers, and seasoned professionals, PyCon Cameroon is a unique opportunity to learn, network, and collaborate with like-minded individuals.
about.missionInvite: We invite you to join us in celebrating the power of Python and its applications in shaping the future of technology in Cameroon and beyond.
about.teamSubtitle: The PyCon Cameroon team is made up of a diverse group of individuals with a wide range of experiences and skills. We are committed to providing a supportive and inclusive environment for all attendees, speakers, sponsors, and volunteers.
about.volunteerText: Interested in joining the organizing team? We're always looking for passionate volunteers!
about.partnersSubtitle: We are proud to collaborate with these amazing communities across Africa and beyond.
about.conductText: PyCon Cameroon is dedicated to providing a harassment-free conference experience for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, age, sexual orientation, disability, physical appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, religion (or lack thereof), or technology choices.
about.conductP1: We do not tolerate harassment of conference participants in any form. Sexual language and imagery is not appropriate for any conference venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter, and other online media.
about.conductP2: Conference participants violating these rules may be sanctioned or expelled from the conference without a refund at the discretion of the conference organizers.

speakers.title: Call for
speakers.subtitle: Share your expertise and inspire the Python community in Cameroon
speakers.excitedText: Whether you're a first-time speaker or a seasoned presenter, we want to hear from you! If you have an interesting Python topic, project, or experience to share, submit your proposal and we'll review it with care.
speakers.bannerInvite: We're inviting speakers of all experience levels and backgrounds to contribute to our conference program. Don't be shy – your unique perspective matters!
speakers.cfpUpcoming: Call for Proposals will be OPEN on {{date}}!
speakers.cfpOpen: Call for Proposals is NOW OPEN!
speakers.cfpClosed: Call for Proposals is now CLOSED. Thank you for your submissions!
speakers.ctaUpcoming: We can't wait to see your proposal. Submissions open {{date}}!
speakers.ctaOpen: We can't wait to see your proposal. Submit before {{date}}!
speakers.ctaClosed: The CFP is closed. Stay tuned for speaker announcements!
speakers.guidelinesSubtitle: Everything you need to know to submit a successful proposal
speakers.guidelinesIntro: We are very excited to invite everyone to PyCon Cameroon 2026! We are seeking speakers of all experience levels and backgrounds to contribute to our conference program. If you use Python professionally, as a hobbyist, or are just excited about Python or programming and open source communities, we'd love to hear from you.
speakers.guidelinesNote: Please take the time to read the information below to help you submit an informed and successful proposal.
speakers.regularTalksText: Standard conference talks covering Python topics, case studies, best practices, and technical deep-dives.
speakers.lightningTalksText: Short, focused presentations on a single topic, idea, or project. Great for first-time speakers!
speakers.workshopsSessionsText: Hands-on tutorials where attendees learn by doing. Includes coding exercises and practical projects.
speakers.panelDiscussionsText: Moderated discussions with multiple experts on trending Python topics and industry trends.
speakers.ubuconTrackText: In partnership with UbuCon Cameroon, we are accepting proposals for our dedicated Ubuntu & open source track. Topics include Ubuntu development, Linux system administration, open source contributions, cloud infrastructure, and Python in the Ubuntu ecosystem. Select "UbuCon Track" when submitting on Sessionize.
speakers.aiPolicyText: Proposal submissions that are solely or largely written by AI tools (such as ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) will not be accepted. Your proposal should reflect your own thoughts and expertise.
proposalLimitItems: Each individual may submit a maximum of two proposals
Each proposal may include a maximum of two speakers
Co-speaking counts toward your submission limit
speakers.recordingNoticeText: All PyCon Cameroon talks and workshops will be recorded and released on our YouTube channel to help with Python education across Africa. By submitting, you agree to be recorded.
speakers.speakerSupportText: Please note that PyCon Cameroon is currently unable to provide financial assistance (travel, accommodation, etc.) to speakers. All selected speakers will receive a free conference ticket for the full event.
speakers.cocP1: All speakers are expected to have read and adhere to the conference Code of Conduct. Slide contents and spoken material should be appropriate for a professional audience including people of many different backgrounds.
speakers.cocP2: Sexual language and imagery are not appropriate, and neither is language or imagery that denigrates or demeans people based on race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, physical appearance, disability, or any other dimension of diversity.
speakers.featuredSubtitle: Meet some of our confirmed speakers for PyCon Cameroon 2026
speakers.comingSoonText: Our speaker lineup is currently being finalized. Check back soon to see the amazing speakers joining us at PyCon Cameroon 2026!
speakers.reachAudienceText: Share your knowledge with the growing Python community in Cameroon and Central Africa.
speakers.recordedSharedText: Your talk will be recorded and shared on YouTube, reaching even more developers worldwide.
speakers.freePassText: Selected speakers receive a complimentary conference pass for both days of PyCon Cameroon.
speakers.speakerNetworkingText: Connect with fellow speakers, industry leaders, and passionate developers from across Africa.
speakers.buildBrandText: Establish yourself as an expert in your field and boost your professional profile.
speakers.giveBackText: Help grow the Python community and inspire the next generation of African developers.

sponsor.title: Become a
sponsor.subtitle: Join us in shaping the future of Python in Cameroon and Central Africa
sponsor.whySponsorText: PyCon Cameroon brings together developers, engineers, data scientists, and tech enthusiasts from across East and Central Africa for an unforgettable celebration of Python programming.
sponsor.connectTalentText: Engage directly with hundreds of passionate Python developers, from beginners to experts.
sponsor.brandAwarenessText: Showcase your company to a highly engaged technical audience in the growing African tech ecosystem.
sponsor.supportCommunityText: Help foster the growth of Python programming in Cameroon and contribute to technological advancement in the region.
sponsor.recruitmentText: Meet potential candidates face-to-face in a dynamic, technology-focused environment.
sponsor.tiersSubtitle: Choose the package that best fits your goals
sponsor.pricesLocal: Prices are listed in XAF (Cameroon Franc). USD equivalent shown for reference.
sponsor.pricesInternational: Prices are listed in USD. Payment in local currency (XAF) is also accepted.
sponsor.customPackagesText: Don't see a package that fits your needs? We're happy to discuss custom sponsorship opportunities!
sponsor.getInvolvedText: Join companies that are leading the way in supporting the Python community in Africa.

attend.title: Attend
attend.subtitle: Everything you need to know about registration, travel, and your stay in Yaoundé
attend.registrationSubtitle: Register today to secure your spot at Cameroon's premier Python event
attend.registrationText: PyCon Cameroon trusts you to self-select a fair registration rate. By choosing the appropriate rate, you help keep the conference affordable for students and those requiring financial assistance.
attend.earlyBirdText: Secure your spot early at our special discounted rate. Available for all attendees for a limited time.
attend.normalRateText: Standard ticket price for the two-day conference, including lunch, refreshments, and conference swag.
attend.onSiteText: Purchase your ticket on-site during the conference. Subject to availability — we recommend buying in advance!
attend.accessTo: Your registration gives you access to:
mainConferenceItems: Access to all keynote sessions and talk tracks
Access to the Expo Hall and Job Fair
attend.paymentSupportText: Your registration helps us keep PyCon Cameroon affordable. Costs go towards:
paymentItems: Travel grants for those who need assistance
attend.visaText: Most visitors to Cameroon require a visa. Please check with your local Cameroonian embassy or consulate for specific requirements.
attend.visaLettersText: We can provide an official invitation letter to support your visa application once you have a paid registration.
attend.cancellationText: Cancellations received by August 15, 2026, will receive a refund less a processing fee. No refunds will be granted after this date unless for visa denial or health reasons.
attend.ticketTransfersText: You may transfer your ticket to another person by sending a written request to organizers@pythoncameroon.org before September 1, 2026.

venue.title: The
venue.subtitle: Join us in the beautiful city of Yaoundé, the political capital of Cameroon
venue.finalizingVenue: We are currently finalizing the perfect venue to host PyCon Cameroon 2026. We're looking for a space that embodies the spirit of our community—modern, accessible, and vibrant.
venue.accommodationText: We will be partnering with several hotels near the conference venue to offer special rates for attendees.
venue.hotelsSoon: List coming soon once venue is confirmed!
venue.accommodationRange: Yaoundé offers a range of accommodation options from luxury hotels to budget-friendly guesthouses.
venue.byAirText: Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport (NSI) is the main airport serving the city.
venue.byBusText: Several reliable bus companies operate routes connecting Yaoundé to other major cities like Douala.
venue.rideHailing: Ride-hailing apps like Yango and local taxis are widely available for getting around the city.
venue.exploreSubtitle: Enjoy the city of seven hills during your stay

privacy.title: Privacy and
privacy.subtitle: How we handle your data
privacy.summary: The Python Software Community in Cameroon only collects the information necessary to register you for PyCon Cameroon and to provide you access to PyCon Cameroon content and services. Registrant contact information is NOT shared with third parties without your explicit opt-in consent.
privacy.submitTalk: If you submit a talk, tutorial, or other content, your name will be publicly associated with your personal content for purposes of giving you credit for your contribution to the conference and to maintain historical records.
privacy.optIn: You may opt in to getting email messages about the PyCon Cameroon conference, and you may opt in to sharing basic contact information with sponsors. Choosing not to opt in will in no way detract from your ability to attend or engage with PyCon Cameroon.
privacy.whoResponsibleTitle: Who Is Responsible For The Personal Information We Collect?
privacy.whoResponsibleP1: For the purpose of data protection laws, the Python Software Community in Cameroon is the data controller in respect to the personal information that we collect and use as part of our operations. As the data controller, we control, manage, and dictate the purpose for which your personal information is used and how we use your personal information for our operation. Only our system administrators and our support team have access to your data, and we ensure that they comply with relevant data protection requirements in their volunteer work. You can amend and remove any information at any time from your profile page on the conference website.
privacy.whoResponsibleP2: If you choose to provide your personal information to other parties during PyCon Cameroon, including sponsors, then their data privacy policies apply to the data that you give to them.
privacy.whatCollectTitle: What Personal Information Do We Collect?
privacy.whatCollectIntro: The type of personal information that we may collect includes, but is not limited to, the following:
whatCollectItems: Company, firm, organisation, agency, or other entity information
Preference information, such as communications that you receive from us
Information that you provide to us for the purposes of attending meetings, events, webinars, and other programmes
Any other information relating to you (or other individuals) which you may provide to us
privacy.whatCollectThirdParty: If you provide personal information to us regarding or related to a third party, you confirm that you have the consent of the third party to share such personal information and that you have made the information in this Privacy Policy available to the relevant third party.
privacy.whatCollectUse: We use the information you give us to create and find badges, track helpdesk questions, write invoices, show your profile (if made public), contact you with updates regarding the conference, and provide you with access to announcements for subsequent events. For speakers, we may also use the information in the conference booklet, the schedule, and on web pages of the conference website.
privacy.whatCollectDemographics: Some of the information is also used to create demographics for the conference in anonymous and aggregated form, which we use for advertising the conference to potential sponsors.
privacy.linksText: Our website may contain links to other websites of interest. However, once you have used these links to leave our site, we do not have any control over that other website. Therefore, we cannot be responsible for the protection and privacy of any information which you provide whilst visiting such sites, which are not governed by this privacy statement. You should exercise caution and look at the privacy statement applicable to the website in question.
privacy.howUseTitle: How Do We Use The Personal Information We Collect?
privacy.howUseIntro: The Python Software Community in Cameroon collects personal information for the following principal purposes:
howUseItems: To provide the PyCon Cameroon website to you
To enrol you for PyCon Cameroon-related mailings (opt-in only)
To register you for events, tutorials, and other services
To provide you with information about Python, PyCon Cameroon, and events within the conference
To allow you to connect with PyCon Cameroon sponsors (opt-in only)
To communicate with you and to respond to your questions, inquiries, or concerns
To administer and improve PyCon Cameroon, including the website, events, tutorials, and other services
To process your payment for PyCon Cameroon services and events
To protect and enforce our legal rights
privacy.howUseLegitimate: We do this for our legitimate business purposes, and under certain circumstances, to perform a contract between you and us. We may request your consent in circumstances where a legal justification over and above legitimate interests is required by applicable law.
privacy.audioText: We will have audio and video recordings during the event (for example, talk recordings), as well as one or more photographers taking pictures to help us with marketing the conference. We may publish these photos and recordings on our websites or third-party sites such as YouTube and social media, and in other marketing material such as brochures, flyers, and promotional videos. We will take care not to make public photos, videos, or audio recordings which could cause harm to the individuals shown or heard in them. If you find such material on any of our sites or accounts, please write to privacy@pyconcameroon.org to ask for review.
privacy.rightsTitle: Your Rights, Including Removing, Correcting or Updating Personal Information
privacy.rightsP1: If you change your mind on the use of your information or wish to access, correct, or update personal information (such as your address), we will endeavour to correct, update, or remove the personal data that you give us. Information that you have explicitly made available for unrestricted public access, such as having your name associated with a talk that you provide, may not be able to be deleted.
privacy.rightsP2: Cameroon's Law No. 2010/012 on Cybersecurity and Cybercriminality, alongside broader data protection principles, provides individuals with rights to control their personal data, including:
rightsItems: Right of access to your personal information
Right to rectify your personal information
Right to erasure of your personal information
Right to restrict the use of your personal information
Right to object to the personal use of your information
Right to complain to the relevant authority
privacy.rightsException: An exception to data wiping is invoices and accounting data: if you have purchased something on our website, we are legally required to preserve the invoices for at least 6 years after the conference.
privacy.retentionTitle: How Long Will We Keep Your Personal Information?
privacy.retentionText: We will keep your personal information for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed.
privacy.questionsText: If you have any questions, concerns, or complaints about this Privacy Policy, please contact privacy@pyconcameroon.org. You are entitled to make an anonymous complaint or inquiry in relation to this Privacy Policy or your privacy rights; however, we may require you to identify yourself if required by law or if it is impracticable for us to deal with your matter otherwise. We will acknowledge receipt of any complaint and will strive to provide you with a written response within 30 days of receipt of your complaint.
privacy.updatesTitle: Informing You About Updates to This Policy
privacy.updatesText: We may update this policy periodically to add new services or to adapt it to new regulations. In order to keep you up to date with changes, we will issue notices on our website and news channels, and in some cases send you an email explaining the change. In case of a possible data breach, we will try to contact you directly via email if we believe that your data was affected.
privacy.organizedBy: PyCon Cameroon 2026 is organised by a team of volunteers on behalf of the Python community in Cameroon.

terms.title: Terms and
terms.subtitle: Please read these terms carefully before registering
terms.introText: Welcome to PyCon Cameroon 2026. By registering for or attending our conference, you agree to comply with and be bound by the following terms and conditions.
regItems: All registrations must be paid in full prior to the event.
Tickets are transferable until August 1, 2026, upon written request.
We reserve the right to cancel any registration that does not comply with our policies.
terms.cancelText: Cancellations received by July 15, 2026, will receive a refund less a processing fee. No refunds will be granted after this date unless for visa denial or serious health reasons, subject to verification.
terms.cocText: All attendees, speakers, sponsors, and volunteers are required to agree with the Code of Conduct. Organizers will enforce this code throughout the event. We expect cooperation from all participants to help ensure a safe environment for everybody.
terms.photoText: By attending PyCon Cameroon, you grant permission for the organizers to use photographs and video recordings of you taken during the event for marketing and promotional purposes.
terms.liabilityText: PyCon Cameroon organizers will not be liable for any loss, injury, or damage to persons or property during the event. Attendees are responsible for their own personal belongings and safety.
terms.contactText: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at

healthSafety.title: Health and Safety
healthSafety.subtitle: PyCon Cameroon 2026
healthSafety.intro: PyCon Cameroon 2026 will be held as a live, in-person event in Yaoundé, Cameroon. Your health and safety are important to us. We want PyCon Cameroon to be an event that everyone feels safe attending. PyCon Cameroon will follow applicable national and local health and safety laws, adapting our plans accordingly and sharing updates with all attendees.
healthSafety.maskP1: We are asking you to wear a mask during your time at PyCon Cameroon. That includes talks, the expo hall, hallways, and sprints.
maskExceptionItems: Indoors while actively consuming food or beverages
While necessary for communicating with someone who is hearing impaired when the ability to see the mouth is essential for communication
healthSafety.maskRequirements: Masks must be worn over the nose and mouth and must be made of a tight-knit, non-permeable material. N95 or equivalent masks such as KN95 or K95 are recommended. Alternatives to face coverings such as face shields, loose gaiters, scarves, bandanas, and face coverings with ventilation are not permitted to be used as masks. Although we encourage you to bring your own masks that you find comfortable, there will also be free masks available for attendees during each day of PyCon Cameroon, as supplies permit.
healthSafety.maskEnforcement: Attendees who are repeatedly unwilling to comply with the mask requirements will be asked to leave and are not eligible for a refund.
healthSafety.vaccP1: We encourage PyCon Cameroon attendees to stay up to date with the vaccinations that are available to them where they live. We know that attendees are coming from different regions and countries, and the availability of vaccines may vary. We encourage you to do your best.
healthSafety.vaccP2: We expect people to stay home if they may be contagious. If you are feeling sick or exhibiting symptoms of COVID-19, or test positive for COVID-19, prior to the start of the conference or on any day of the conference, please contact us at organizers@pyconcameroon.org and our health and safety team will advise accordingly.
healthSafety.visaP1: Cameroon visa requirements vary depending on your nationality. Citizens of ECOWAS member states and several other African countries may enter Cameroon without a visa or obtain a visa on arrival. Citizens of most other countries will need to obtain a visa from a Cameroonian Embassy or Consulate prior to travel.
healthSafety.visaP2: Please check your specific country's requirements well in advance and contact us if you require a support letter for your visa application. You should also ensure you have any required vaccination certificates (such as yellow fever) before travelling to Cameroon.
healthSafety.airportText: All international travel will arrive through Nsimalen International Airport, Yaoundé. Various airlines provide services to Yaoundé. Some hotels offer pick-up services and we encourage participants with such options to use them. Taxis and ride-hailing services are also available.
healthSafety.adviceIntro: Please read this information carefully. Some of it is crucial and it will help you have a better travel experience.

adviceItems.label: Visa:
adviceItems.text: You may need a visa to enter Cameroon. Check this well in advance, as failing to do so may result in denied entry or being unable to board your flight. Contact us if you have any questions.

adviceItems.label: Yellow Fever Certificate:
adviceItems.text: A valid yellow fever vaccination certificate is required to enter Cameroon. Please ensure you have this before travelling — do not leave it until the last minute.

adviceItems.label: Other vaccinations:
adviceItems.text: Optional but recommended vaccinations include Hepatitis A, Tetanus, and Typhoid.

adviceItems.label: Malaria precautions:
adviceItems.text: Malaria precautions are strongly advised. Seek medical advice on whether you should be taking anti-malarial medication before and during your visit.

healthSafety.habitsP1: Hand sanitizing stations will be placed throughout PyCon Cameroon venues to encourage frequent hand sanitization.
healthSafety.habitsP2: We will issue best practices for attendees that include avoiding close-contact greetings where possible and how exhibitors can best manage product demonstrations.
healthSafety.disclaimerText: PyCon Cameroon takes these Health and Safety Guidelines very seriously and will enforce a 3-strike rule. Anyone who violates these guidelines on three separate occasions — such as continuing to not wear a proper mask or disregarding safe distancing — is subject to removal from the conference. Anyone who provides false vaccination verification will not be admitted to PyCon Cameroon.
healthSafety.organizedBy: PyCon Cameroon 2026 is organised by a team of volunteers on behalf of the Python community in Cameroon.

codeOfConduct.title: Code of
codeOfConduct.subtitle: PyCon Cameroon 2026
codeOfConduct.commitmentP1: PyCon Cameroon is dedicated to providing a harassment-free conference experience for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, age, sexual orientation, disability, physical appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, religion (or lack thereof), or technology choices.
codeOfConduct.commitmentP2: We do not tolerate harassment of conference participants in any form. Sexual language and imagery is not appropriate for any conference venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter, and other online media.
codeOfConduct.commitmentP3: Conference participants violating these rules may be sanctioned or expelled from the conference without a refund at the discretion of the conference organizers.
expectedItems: Refrain from demeaning, discriminatory or harassing behavior and speech.
Be mindful of your surroundings and of your fellow participants. Alert conference organizers if you notice a dangerous situation or someone in distress.
Participate in an authentic and active way. In doing so, you help to create PyCon Cameroon and make it your own.
codeOfConduct.unacceptableIntro: Unacceptable behaviors include, but are not limited to:
unacceptableItems: Intimidating, harassing, abusive, discriminatory, derogatory, or demeaning conduct by any attendees of PyCon Cameroon and related events.
Offensive verbal comments related to gender, sexual orientation, race, religion, or disability.
Inappropriate use of nudity and/or sexual images in public spaces (including presentation slides).
Sustained disruption of talks or other events.
codeOfConduct.consequencesP1: Unacceptable behavior will not be tolerated whether by other attendees, media, speakers, volunteers, organizers, venue staff, sponsors, or exhibitors.
codeOfConduct.consequencesP2: Anyone asked to stop unacceptable behavior is expected to comply immediately.
codeOfConduct.consequencesP3: If a participant engages in unacceptable behavior, the conference organizers may take any action they deem appropriate, up to and including expulsion from the conference without warning or refund.
codeOfConduct.witnessTitle: What to Do if You Witness or Are Subject to Unacceptable Behavior
codeOfConduct.witnessP1: If you are subject to unacceptable behavior, notice that someone else is being subject to unacceptable behavior, or have any other concerns, please notify a conference organizer as soon as possible.
codeOfConduct.witnessP2: The PyCon Cameroon team will be available to help participants contact venue security or local law enforcement, to provide escorts, or to otherwise assist those experiencing unacceptable behavior to feel safe for the duration of the conference.
codeOfConduct.contactText: If you need to report any issues, please reach out to us:

ubucon.headerSubtitle: A dedicated Ubuntu & open source track at PyCon Cameroon 2026
ubucon.coOrganized: Co-organized by Python Cameroon & UbuCon Cameroon
ubucon.whatIsP1: UbuCon (Ubuntu Conference) is a community-driven event that brings together Ubuntu enthusiasts, developers, and open source advocates. For the first time in Cameroon, we're co-hosting a UbuCon track within PyCon Cameroon 2026.
ubucon.whatIsP2: This partnership between Python Cameroon and UbuCon Cameroon celebrates the deep relationship between Python and the Ubuntu ecosystem — from system tools and automation to cloud infrastructure and beyond.
ubucon.topicsSubtitle: What you can expect at the UbuCon track

topics.title: Ubuntu & Python
topics.text: How Python powers Ubuntu development, packaging, system tools, and automation. From apt to Juju, Python is everywhere in Ubuntu.

topics.title: Linux System Administration
topics.text: Managing Ubuntu servers, desktop environments, networking, and security best practices for developers and sysadmins.

topics.title: Open Source Contributions
topics.text: Getting started with open source, contributing to Ubuntu and upstream projects, and building a portfolio through FOSS.

topics.title: Cloud & Infrastructure
topics.text: Ubuntu Server, LXD, MicroK8s, cloud-native development, and deploying Python applications on Ubuntu infrastructure.

topics.title: Snap & Packaging
topics.text: Distributing Python applications as Snaps, building packages for Ubuntu, and understanding the Linux packaging ecosystem.

topics.title: IoT & Embedded
topics.text: Ubuntu Core, Python on embedded devices, IoT solutions, and building smart devices with Ubuntu and Python.

ubucon.ctaText: Whether you're an Ubuntu power user or just getting started with Linux, the UbuCon track has something for you.

financialAid.title: Financial Aid
financialAid.subtitle: PyCon Cameroon 2026
financialAid.aboutText: We are delighted you are interested in attending PyCon Cameroon 2026, which will be held in Yaoundé, Cameroon. As part of our commitment to fostering an inclusive community, we recognize that some individuals may need financial assistance to make conference participation possible.
financialAid.grantText: Due to budget limitations, we can only support a small number of attendees with financial aid grants. These grants will be awarded based on financial need and responses provided in the application form. Please note that approved grants may only cover certain expenses (e.g. conference ticket but not accommodation). Any expenses not covered by your specific grant would remain your responsibility.
financialAid.applicationP1: Please complete the form to provide details on your financial needs and circumstances. This information is essential for the grants review committee to evaluate applicants and make fair and informed decisions on grant recipients.
financialAid.applicationP2: We welcome your open and honest responses, even for basic needs. Our goal is to make the conference accessible to as many Python enthusiasts as possible.
financialAid.applicationP3: Your application will be treated in complete confidence.
financialAid.whoP1: We'd love to be able to help all those who submit applications, but our funds are not unlimited, so sadly we won't be able to meet all requests.
financialAid.whoP2: The financial assistance programme will prioritise:
whoItems: Those who will contribute back to their local communities
Those who will help increase diversity at the event and in Cameroonian and African programming
financialAid.whoP3: We will do our best to maximise the impact of our financial assistance funding.
financialAid.howP1: Please complete our form before the application deadline (to be announced). We will not accept applications after the stated date.
financialAid.howP2: Read the questions on the form carefully and answer them as best you can.
financialAid.whatRequestText: You can request a free ticket only, or a grant as well as a free ticket. Anyone who is allocated a grant will automatically receive a free ticket.
financialAid.howMuchP1: Our budget is limited, and we will have to make some hard decisions. Generally, it is easier for us to accept smaller requests, as a single large request could consume most of our budget. The lower your request, the more likely it is that we will be able to accept it.
howMuchItems: How much you will need to spend in total in order to attend the conference
How much of this you can contribute yourself
financialAid.howMuchP3: The difference between these two figures is the amount you should request.
financialAid.calculateTitle: How Should I Calculate How Much to Request?
financialAid.calculateIntro: You will need to budget for:
calculateItems: Subsistence (evening meals, local transport, etc.) while in Yaoundé
financialAid.travelText: Spend a little time researching your options. If you will need to travel by air, use websites such as Skyscanner to estimate the cost of airfare. Be aware that a little flexibility with dates or routes can make a huge difference to the price of an air ticket.
financialAid.accommodationP1: There are many websites that will allow you to estimate the cost of accommodation, such as Hotels.com or Booking.com. If you know someone else who is also travelling, you can share a room to lower costs. If you are willing to share a room, let us know in your application.
financialAid.accommodationP2: Be reasonable — we want you to stay somewhere clean, safe, and comfortable, but we cannot afford to place you in a luxury hotel!
financialAid.subsistenceText: You will have lunch and refreshments each day as part of the conference. In Yaoundé, you can get a decent evening meal for a very affordable price. Local transport by taxi or moto-taxi is also inexpensive.
financialAid.timelineIntro: Exact dates will be announced — the structure will follow this general pattern:
timelineItems: Financial aid application opens: To be announced
Financial aid application closes: To be announced
Financial aid grants awarded: To be announced
Deadline to accept/decline financial aid: To be announced
Disbursement sessions at PyCon Cameroon: At the conference venue, Yaoundé
financialAid.fundsTitle: When Will I Receive the Funds?
financialAid.fundsP1: Funds will be provided to successful applicants on attendance at the conference.
financialAid.fundsP2: Unfortunately, applicants for financial assistance who have received funds in advance and have failed to attend have damaged previous events. Our financial situation cannot withstand this kind of abuse.
financialAid.fundsP3: In exceptional circumstances, we may be able to provide funds in advance to those who would otherwise find it impossible to attend. Please contact us as soon as possible if this is the case.
financialAid.organizedBy: PyCon Cameroon 2026 is organised by a team of volunteers on behalf of the Python community in Cameroon.

ubucon.title: Ubuntu & Python
ubucon.text: How Python powers Ubuntu development, packaging, and automation.

ubucon.title: Open Source Contributions
ubucon.text: Getting started with contributing to Ubuntu and open source projects.

ubucon.title: Cloud & Infrastructure
ubucon.text: Ubuntu Server, cloud-native development, and DevOps with Python.

ubucon.title: Community & Networking
ubucon.text: Meet the Ubuntu Cameroon community and connect with global contributors.

django.title: Django Web Development
django.text: Building modern web applications with Django and best practices.

django.title: REST APIs & DRF
django.text: Designing and building APIs with Django REST Framework.

django.title: Deployment & Scaling
django.text: Production deployment, performance optimization, and scaling strategies.

django.title: Django & AI/ML
django.text: Integrating machine learning models and AI features into Django apps.

values.title: Building a Welcoming Community
values.text: We believe that diversity and inclusivity are essential for the growth and success of our community. Everyone is welcome here, regardless of background, experience level, or identity.

values.title: Inspiring Talks
values.text: From beginner tutorials to advanced deep dives, we offer a wide range of talks to cater to the needs of our diverse community. Learn from local and international Python experts.

values.title: Practical Workshops
values.text: We learn by doing. Our hands-on workshops are designed to help you build real skills that you can apply immediately in your projects and career.

values.title: Simple and All-Inclusive
values.text: We want to create a welcoming and inclusive environment for everyone, regardless of their background or experience. No gatekeeping, just community.

values.title: Innovation & Creativity
values.text: We encourage creative problem-solving and innovative thinking. PyCon Cameroon is a space where new ideas are born and nurtured.

values.title: Growing Together
values.text: Whether you're mentoring others or learning yourself, we believe in the power of shared growth. Together, we're building the future of Python in Cameroon.

venues.name: National Museum
venues.description: Explore the rich history and culture of Cameroon in this former presidential palace.

venues.name: Bois Sainte Anastasie
venues.description: A beautiful urban park perfect for a relaxing walk amidst nature in the city center.

venues.name: Monument de la Réunification
venues.description: An iconic monument symbolizing the reunification of French and British Cameroons.

venues.name: Palais Charles Atangana
venues.description: Historic colonial-era palace of a paramount chief, now a cultural heritage landmark in Yaoundé.

venues.name: Mvog-Betsi Zoo
venues.description: Yaoundé's zoo and botanical garden, home to native wildlife including primates and tropical birds.

venues.name: Lac Municipal de Yaoundé
venues.description: A scenic artificial lake in the city center, popular for walks, boat rides, and relaxation.

venues.name: Eco Park Yaoundé
venues.description: A green leisure park offering nature trails, picnic areas, and outdoor activities for families.

venues.name: Basilique Marie-Reine-des-Apôtres
venues.description: Striking hilltop basilica with panoramic views, one of Central Africa's most impressive churches.

venues.name: Dade Park
venues.description: A popular recreational park in Yaoundé with green spaces, playgrounds, and weekend entertainment.
